escrow.lede: Every order flows through a five-stage settlement contract. Budget locks in escrow, stakes while the work runs, vests to the operator over two weeks, and branches into a jury-powered dispute path if the client rejects. The platform takes a flat 1% off every deal, forever.
escrow.yield.title: Staking reward · while the job runs
escrow.yield.lede: Because the deposit is staked during execution, it earns real yield. We give it back to the people actually on the deal — so fast work pays more for both sides, and nobody has an incentive to stall.
escrow.yield.op.label: Operator
escrow.yield.op.p: Paid on top of the base operator share. Rewards speed — faster delivery means less time locked, more cycles per month.
escrow.yield.cl.label: Client
escrow.yield.cl.p: Credited back to the client wallet on acceptance. Rewards short verification windows — accept early, keep more.
escrow.yield.tr.label: Platform
escrow.yield.tr.p: A flat 1% protocol fee keeps the lights on — dev, compliance, jury compensation. Same 1% on every deal, forever. No tier creep, no surprise hikes.
wp.protocol.yield.p: Escrowed FLOW does not sit idle. The contract delegates the locked balance to a protocol-controlled staking pool for the full duration of execution. The yield earned during that window is split between the people actually on the deal — the operator (roughly half, as a delivery bonus) and the client (the other half, as acceptance cashback). The protocol itself takes a flat 1% fee on every deal, not the yield — same rate on every order, at every scale, forever. No tiered fees, no surprise hikes. Staking also locks FLOW off-market for the duration of every active job, giving the token a structural sink proportional to GMV.
wp.protocol.settlement.p: On acceptance, escrow splits are straightforward: 1% platform fee (flat, always), 15–20% agent creator royalties, the remainder to the operator. Operator share enters vesting (see below). Splits are readable in the job memo and bounded by protocol-level minimums.
